result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Render a directory structure similar to the Windows tree command

USAGE:
    arbor tree [path] [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    arbor tree
    arbor tree -f
    arbor tree --depth 2

ARGUMENTS:
    [path]    Root directory to enumerate. Defaults to the current working      
              directory                                                         

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                     Prints help information                      
    -f, --files                    Include files in the tree output (similar to 
                                   `tree /f`)                                   
        --depth <LEVEL>            Limit traversal depth. 0 shows only the root,
                                   1 includes direct children, etc              
        --ascii                    Render connectors using ASCII characters     
                                   (similar to `tree /a`)                       
    -d, --details                  Display default metadata (size and timestamp)
        --details-field <FIELD>    Include specific metadata fields (size, time,
                                   perm, owner, group). Supports comma-separated
                                   values                                       
        --include <GLOB>           Include only files matching the provided glob
                                   pattern (supports multiple)                  
        --exclude <GLOB>           Exclude files matching the provided glob     
                                   pattern (supports multiple)                  
    -e, --ext <EXTENSION>          Filter files by extension, e.g. `--ext .cs`  
                                   or `--ext json` (supports multiple)          
        --include-dir <GLOB>       Include only directories matching the        
                                   provided glob pattern (supports multiple)    
        --exclude-dir <GLOB>       Exclude directories matching the provided    
                                   glob pattern (supports multiple)             
        --export <FORMAT>          Choose the export format: text, json, or     
                                   markdown                                     
        --output <PATH>            Write the exported tree to a file instead of 
                                   stdout
